by_judge.judge: P SREE SUDHA IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 19.06.2026

narrative:
On June 19, 2026, the Karnataka High Court achieved a 49.9% overall hearing rate, resolving 1,125 out of 2,256 scheduled cases. This represents a notable efficiency improvement compared to the preceding days, rising from 44.0% on June 18 and 43.5% on June 17, despite a general contraction in the total scheduled case volume from a weekly high of 2,760 cases on June 16.

Performance highlights among individual judges were led by Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19, who heard 121 of 197 scheduled cases (61.4%). In Hall 14, Justice M. Nagaprasanna managed a heavy multi-session load, collectively hearing 126 of 269 scheduled matters. Justice V. Srishananda in Hall 21 converted 44 of 54 scheduled cases (81.5%) in his main session. Conversely, Justice B. M. Shyam Prasad's largest session of 104 scheduled cases yielded only 8 hearings (7.7%), though his smaller listings achieved conversion rates as high as 88.2%.

In terms of hearing stages, "Orders" remained the most prominent category with 843 scheduled and 453 heard, yielding a 53.7% conversion rate—an increase from the 48.0% recorded on June 18. "Final Hearings" also showed upward progress, rising to a 31.1% hearing rate from 22.1% the previous day, while "Admission" matters saw a 46.4% clearance rate.

Writ Petitions (WP) continued to dominate the court's docket with 910 scheduled and 410 heard (45.1%), reflecting a minor volume reduction but an efficiency increase from June 18's 43.2% rate. Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) followed with 388 scheduled and 198 heard (51.0%), while Regular Second Appeals (RSA) registered a high conversion rate of 56.7%, clearing 80 of 141 scheduled cases.
